title: Ordering Food in Thai — Your First 8 Phrases

vocab.explanation: This means 'to order' — you use it anytime you're ordering food, drinks, anything at a restaurant or cafe. It goes at the beginning of the phrase, before what you're ordering. You'll hear this word a lot because Thai people eat out constantly — street food, markets, restaurants.

exampleSentences.thai: สั่งกาแฟหนึ่งแก้วค่ะ
exampleSentences.note: สั่ง comes first, then what you're ordering, then the quantity.

vocab.explanation: This is how you say 'delicious'. Thai people use it all the time — after every meal, every snack. If someone cooks for you or you eat at their stall, saying อร่อย is the best compliment you can give. You'll often hear it with มาก — อร่อยมาก — which means 'very delicious'.

exampleSentences.thai: ส้มตำร้านนี้อร่อยมากค่ะ
exampleSentences.translit: sôm-tam ráan níi a-ròoy mâak khâ
exampleSentences.english: The som tam at this place is really delicious.
exampleSentences.note: Put the food first, then อร่อย at the end.

vocab.explanation: This means 'spicy'. Thai food is famous for being spicy, so this is one of the most useful words you'll learn. If you can handle the heat, great! But if you can't, you need to know how to say 'not spicy' — which is our next word.

exampleSentences.thai: อาหารไทยเผ็ดมากค่ะ
exampleSentences.note: เผ็ด goes after the noun, like most Thai adjectives.

vocab.explanation: This is just ไม่ — 'not' — plus เผ็ด — 'spicy'. In Thai, you put ไม่ right before the word you want to negate. So ไม่เผ็ด literally means 'not spicy'. Super simple pattern — and you can use ไม่ with any adjective.

exampleSentences.thai: ขอไม่เผ็ดนะคะ
exampleSentences.english: I'd like it not spicy, please.
exampleSentences.note: ไม่ always goes right before the adjective.

vocab.thaiSplit: เก็บ เงิน
vocab.explanation: Literally this means 'collect money'. เก็บ means 'to collect' and เงิน means 'money'. You use it when you want the bill at a restaurant. Just say เก็บเงิน or เก็บเงินด้วยค่ะ and the server will bring your check.

exampleSentences.thai: เก็บเงินด้วยนะคะ
exampleSentences.english: Could I get the bill, please?
exampleSentences.note: Adding ด้วย makes it softer and more polite.

vocab.explanation: This is a really versatile word. It means 'may I have' or 'I'd like'. You use it at the start of a request — ขอ, then what you want. It's polite and you'll use it constantly. At a restaurant, at a market, at a coffee shop — ขอ is your go-to starter.

exampleSentences.thai: ขอน้ำเปล่าหนึ่งขวดค่ะ
exampleSentences.translit: khǎaw náam plào nùeng khùuat khâ
exampleSentences.english: I'd like one bottle of water, please.
exampleSentences.note: ขอ + what you want + how many + polite particle.

vocab.explanation: This means 'full' — as in 'I'm not hungry anymore'. After a big Thai meal, you say อิ่มแล้ว — 'I'm full now'. แล้ว means 'already' or 'now'. It's a nice way to show you enjoyed the food. Saying อิ่มแล้ว is almost like a thank you.

exampleSentences.thai: อิ่มแล้วค่ะ ขอบคุณนะคะ
exampleSentences.translit: ìm láaew khâ khàawp-khun ná khá
exampleSentences.note: อิ่มแล้ว + polite particle is a complete sentence.

vocab.explanation: This means 'to recommend'. When you don't know what to order, this is the magic phrase. Just ask แนะนำอะไรดีคะ — 'What do you recommend?' — and let the vendor pick for you. Thai street food vendors love it when you ask them this.

exampleSentences.thai: ร้านนี้แนะนำอะไรดีคะ
exampleSentences.translit: ráan níi náe-nam a-rai dii khá
exampleSentences.note: You can add the place name before แนะนำ for specificity.

blocks.speakerNote: Energetic, look to camera. Say the Thai phrase then pause 1 beat.

lines.thai: สั่งอะไรดีคะ

lines.english: What would you like to order?
Today you'll learn 8 phrases to order food in Thai like a local.

blocks.speakerNote: Pick ONE cultural fact to open with. Option A (default): Street food scale — 14,000 vendors, sharing culture. Option B: Tom Yum Kung UNESCO 2024, fifth heritage item. Option C: Thai meals are shared — nobody orders one dish, small spoonfuls from shared plates. Delete the unused options after recording.

lines.english: Bangkok has over 14,000 registered street food vendors. It was named the world's number one street food city. And Thai food culture is built on sharing — you never order just one dish for yourself. Everyone gets rice, and you take small spoonfuls from shared plates. So the vendor just needs to know what you want. And that starts with one phrase:

lines.thai: สั่งอะไรดีคะ

lines.english: By the end of this video, you won't need to point. You'll order in Thai, ask for recommendations, tell them how spicy you want it, and get the bill — all in Thai.

blocks.speakerNote: One word at a time. Say the Thai clearly, pause for the card animation, then the example sentence. Keep energy up — this is the core learning block.

lines.thai: สั่ง
คุณอยากสั่งอะไร
lines.english: What would you like to order?

lines.thai: เผ็ด
ไม่เผ็ด
ไม่เอาเผ็ดนะคะ
อร่อย
อร่อยมาก
ขอ
ขอผัดไทยหนึ่งจานค่ะ
lines.english: I'd like one Pad Thai, please.

lines.thai: เก็บเงิน
เก็บเงินด้วยค่ะ
lines.english: Can I have the bill, please?

lines.thai: แนะนำ
แนะนำอะไรดีคะ
อิ่ม
อิ่มแล้วค่ะ

blocks.speakerNote: Natural conversational speed. No pauses between sentences. Imagine you're actually in the restaurant — ordering, eating, paying.

lines.thai: แนะนำอะไรดีคะ
ขอผัดไทยหนึ่งจานค่ะ
ไม่เอาเผ็ดนะคะ
อร่อยมาก
อิ่มแล้วค่ะ
เก็บเงินด้วยค่ะ

blocks.speakerNote: Slow and clear. Say the Thai, pause 1 beat, then the transliteration will appear, pause 1 beat, then English appears. Give the viewer time to process.

lines.thai: แนะนำอะไรดีคะ
ขอผัดไทยหนึ่งจานค่ะ
lines.english: I'd like one Pad Thai, please.
I'd like one Pad Thai, please.

lines.thai: ไม่เอาเผ็ดนะคะ
อร่อยมาก
เก็บเงินด้วยค่ะ
lines.english: Can I have the bill, please?
Can I have the bill, please?

blocks.speakerNote: Look to camera. Ask the question, then wait 3 seconds in silence.

lines.english: How would you say: 'I'd like one Pad Thai, please'?

lines.thai: ขอผัดไทยหนึ่งจานค่ะ
lines.english: I'd like one Pad Thai, please.
How would you say: 'No spicy, please'?

lines.thai: ไม่เอาเผ็ดนะคะ

lines.english: How would you say: 'Can I have the bill, please'?

lines.thai: เก็บเงินด้วยค่ะ
lines.english: Can I have the bill, please?

blocks.speakerNote: Very slow. Pause 2 seconds between each sentence for the viewer to repeat.

lines.thai: แนะนำอะไรดีคะ

lines.thai: ขอผัดไทยหนึ่งจานค่ะ
lines.thaiSplit: ขอ ผัด ไทย หนึ่ง จาน ค่ะ

lines.thaiSplit: ไม่ เอา เผ็ด นะ คะ

blocks.speakerNote: Quick energy. Flash through the vocab cards.

lines.english: Quick recap — here are your 8 phrases for ordering food in Thai.

blocks.speakerNote: Tease next episode — asking for directions.

lines.english: Next time — asking for directions. You'll learn how to say 'where is...?' and understand the answer.

lines.thai: ...อยู่ที่ไหนคะ

imagePrompts.prompt: A single Thai street food wok viewed from slightly above, centred. Wok mid-stir with a wooden spatula, a small curl of steam rising. Hero colour: deep charcoal wok body, warm orange flame underneath. No people, no stall, no background scenery.
imagePrompts.subject: Thai wok — street food signal

imagePrompts.prompt: A single human hand, palm up, index finger extended and pointing forward, centred. Illustrated simply in flat warm beige. Universal 'I'll have that one' ordering gesture. No menu, no food, no other elements.
imagePrompts.subject: Pointing hand — ordering (สั่ง)

imagePrompts.prompt: A single wide ceramic bowl of pad thai viewed from a 30-degree overhead angle, centred. Flat-illustrated lime wedge to the left, small pile of crushed peanuts to the right. Hero colour: golden orange noodles. White bowl with thin grey rim. No chopsticks, no table, no background.
imagePrompts.subject: Pad thai bowl — delicious (อร่อย)

imagePrompts.prompt: Two whole red Thai bird's eye chillies in a gentle X crossing, centred. Hero colour: vivid flat red. Stems illustrated in flat green. No cutting board, no bowl, no sliced pieces.
imagePrompts.subject: Two crossed chillies — spicy (เผ็ด)

imagePrompts.prompt: A single paper receipt slightly curled at the bottom, centred. The receipt is a plain white rectangle with three short horizontal grey wavy lines (representing print, not readable characters) and a thin line at the base. A simple hand pinches the top between thumb and finger. No wallet, no coins.
imagePrompts.subject: Receipt held in hand — bill please (เก็บเงิน)

imagePrompts.prompt: A single red chilli with a bold flat red X drawn over it, centred. Same chilli illustration style as the spicy image. The X is a thick-stroked crimson overlay. Immediately readable as negation. No bowl, no dish, no food context.
imagePrompts.subject: Chilli with X — not spicy (ไม่เผ็ด)

imagePrompts.prompt: Two hands, palms pressed together in a slight bow — a Thai wai gesture, centred, illustrated flat in warm beige. Hands occupy roughly 60% of frame height. No person above the wrists, no background.
imagePrompts.subject: Wai hands — polite request (ขอ)

imagePrompts.prompt: A single flat-illustrated torso (no head, cropped from shoulder to mid-thigh) with both hands resting gently on the belly in a satisfied posture, centred. Shirt is solid warm coral. Hands cradle a gently rounded belly. No table, no plates, no background.
imagePrompts.subject: Hands on belly — full (อิ่ม)

imagePrompts.prompt: A single flat-illustrated hand with index finger raised in a suggestion gesture (slightly raised and tilted, like making a recommendation), centred. A small flat five-pointed warm yellow star floats above the fingertip. Hero colour: warm amber. No person, no menu, no food.
imagePrompts.subject: Hand with star — recommend (แนะนำ)

imagePrompts.prompt: A single flat magnifying glass centred, glass face toward the viewer. Inside the glass circle: three short horizontal lines of alternating lengths in warm charcoal, suggesting text being examined (not legible characters). Hero colour: warm gold handle and rim. No paper, no desk, no books.
imagePrompts.subject: Magnifying glass — sentence breakdown

imagePrompts.prompt: A single flat speech bubble — rounded rectangle with a small tail pointing lower-left, centred. Inside the bubble: three short parallel sound-wave arcs in soft charcoal, suggesting spoken sound (not text). Hero colour: sky blue bubble body. No person, no books, no desk.
imagePrompts.subject: Speech bubble — shadowing practice

imagePrompts.prompt: A single flat stopwatch viewed face-on, centred. Clock face is mostly white with a simple arc in warm orange showing roughly 3 seconds remaining. Hero colour: orange arc on white face, charcoal body. No pencil, no flashcards, no paper.
imagePrompts.subject: Stopwatch — production drill

imagePrompts.prompt: Eight small flat icons arranged in two rows of four, centred on the canvas with generous spacing between each icon. Icons (each ~15% of total image height): wok, chilli, bowl, pointing hand, receipt, wai hands, speech bubble, satisfied torso silhouette. All icons share the same 3-colour palette. No labels, no numbers, no decorative borders.
imagePrompts.subject: Eight icon grid — recap

imagePrompts.prompt: A single flat illustrated signpost — a wooden post with two arrow-shaped direction signs attached, one pointing left and one pointing right, centred. The signs are completely blank (no text). Hero colour: warm terracotta arrows, charcoal post. No street, no city, no people.
imagePrompts.subject: Blank signpost — teaser for directions
